title: Immediately disabling `fork` from a branch doesn't always work
body:
See test here, which fails:

https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/d864057a607eba75c09093000e053a65a37c1b34/test_regress/t/t_disable_inside.v#L58-L74

Issue seems to be that the `disable` is executed without delay (without suspending the process executing the `disable`), so the second fork branch has no chance of registering itself in the killQueue used to implement the disable.

Probably all it needs is an extra flag next to the killQueue to signal that the `fork` was already disabled, and if so JumpGo to the end of the branch, rather than register itself to the killQueue.

@RRozak in case interested as you added this recently.
